name: Aguallueve and Arguilay Circular Trail
name_original: Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Báguena — circular desde el Castillo de Anento
description: Hike the Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena circular route, a moderate 7.1-mile trail exploring waterfalls, a castle, and a charming
region_intro: Hiking around Anento offers diverse terrain in a picturesque village setting in Zaragoza, Aragon. The region is characterized by unique natural features such as the Aguallueve, a continuously cascading spring forming spectacular karst relief with stone walls and moss. Anento also features historical landmarks like Anento Castle, providing panoramic views of the surrounding area. The landscape combines these natural wonders with varied trails suitable for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Anento The most popular hiking route is Aguallueve and Arguilay Circular Trail, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores waterfalls, the historic castle, and a charming natural environment. Another top favourite among local hikers is Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39), a difficult 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This longer option traverses pine forests and cherry orchards, connecting historic towns. Local hikers also love the Aguallueve de Anento – Anento Castle loop from Anento, a 1.7 miles (2.8 km) trail leading through the unique Aguallueve karst formation and up to the castle, often completed in about 48 minutes. Hiking around Anento is defined by unique karst formations, historical castles, and varied trails through natural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter loops to more challenging treks. The routes in Anento are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Anento's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking around Anento?
A: Anento is renowned for its unique natural features, most notably the &lt;strong&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/strong&gt;. This spectacular spring continuously cascades in droplets, forming an impressive karst relief with walls of stone, lush moss, and hidden small caves. It's a truly distinctive environment for hikers to explore. You can experience this on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974320377/aguallueve-and-arguilay-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve and Arguilay Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or viewpoints to visit on the trails?
A: Yes, Anento offers historical landmarks that provide excellent viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Anento Castle&lt;/strong&gt;, a small rock castle situated on a hill, offers spectacular panoramic views of the surrounding area. Several routes incorporate this, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento – Anento Castle loop from Anento&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca&lt;/a&gt; features the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19812975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca – Puerta Baja de Daroca loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy or short hiking trails suitable for beginners in Anento?
A: Yes, Anento offers options for less experienced hikers or those looking for a shorter walk. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19812975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca – Puerta Baja de Daroca loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.7-mile trail that takes about 54 minutes to complete. For a slightly longer but still manageable option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento – Anento Castle loop from Anento&lt;/a&gt; is 1.7 miles and can be completed in under an hour, offering a glimpse of the Aguallueve and the castle.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Anento?
A: Anento is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is vibrant. Spring brings lush greenery and flowing water at the Aguallueve, while autumn offers beautiful colors. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, and winter can be chilly but often clear.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Anento?
A: Yes, Anento features several circular routes that allow you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974320377/aguallueve-and-arguilay-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve and Arguilay Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.1-mile loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento loop from Anento&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.2 miles long and focuses on the unique karst formations.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Anento?
A: The hiking trails around Anento are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 15 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the unique natural beauty of the Aguallueve, the historical charm of Anento village and its castle, and the varied terrain that offers something for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a greater challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981421202/burbaguena-luco-de-jiloca-trail-pr-te-39" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burbáguena - Luco de Jiloca Trail (PR-TE 39)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.8-mile path. This longer option traverses pine forests and cherry orchards, connecting historic towns and requiring good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Is Anento a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Anento can be suitable for families, especially with its shorter, less strenuous options. The village itself is picturesque, and the unique Aguallueve de Anento is a fascinating natural wonder that can captivate children. Consider shorter loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento – Anento Castle loop from Anento&lt;/a&gt; for an accessible family adventure.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Anento?
A: While specific regulations for each trail can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in rural Spain, provided they are kept under control, especially around livestock or wildlife. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash and carry waste bags. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions in protected areas.

Q: How many hiking routes are there in the Anento area?
A: There are approximately 10 hiking routes available in the Anento area, catering to various skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 5 moderate routes, and 4 more difficult options, ensuring there's a trail for almost every hiker.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink in Anento after a hike?
A: Anento is a small, picturesque village, so dining options are limited but charming. You can typically find a local bar or restaurant within the village itself where you can enjoy traditional Aragonese cuisine and refreshments after your hike. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially outside of peak season.
tips:
We are in the beautiful town of Anento visiting its castle, of which the walls remain, a beautiful main façade with its door and a bridge over a moat.
Nearby, we will find picnic tables shaded by pine trees and beautiful wood carvings/sculptures.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is rated as moderate. While generally suitable for families with children, some sections can be challenging due to steep descents. The clay soil can become slippery, especially after rain, so beginners should exercise caution and wear appropriate footwear.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start on the well-preserved, cobbled streets of Anento village, then move through a lush valley alongside a river. As you ascend, the path can become more arid and aerial, particularly along the ridge to Arguilay. The route also features limestone rock formations, moss, and red clay ravines, especially around the Aguallueve de Anento.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several fascinating spots. Highlights include the charming Anento Castle ruins, offering expansive views, and the unique Aguallueve de Anento, a natural spring with continuously dripping water creating a vibrant microclimate. The route also passes by the Arguilay Recreation Area and the interesting erosive formations of Aguallueve de Arguilay.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: The hike starts from the Castillo de Anento, which is located in the village of Anento. You can typically find parking within or near the village, which serves as the trailhead for this circular route.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied terrain, including cobbled streets, potentially slippery sections, and areas with diverse wildlife, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with moderate hikes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but each season offers a different experience. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors. In winter, the Aguallueve de Anento can form impressive ice stalactites, but paths might be more slippery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular route around Anento. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The diverse habitats along the trail support various wildlife, including blackbirds, cuckoos, and even roe deer. The vegetation ranges from riverbank forests with poplars and pines in the valleys to more arid plants in higher, open areas. The Aguallueve de Anento area is particularly rich in mosses and ferns.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Rambla de Arguilay, Rambla de Anento, and the Senda del Aguallueve. It also briefly touches on the Ruta 11. PR-Z 51. Nombrevilla - Anento.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms available near the trail?
faqs.answer: The village of Anento, where the trail starts and ends, is known for its charm and offers some amenities. You can find cafes and potentially other services within the village. The Arguilay Recreation Area also provides facilities along the route.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, wear comfortable hiking boots with good grip, especially since paths can be slippery after rain. Layers of clothing are advisable for changing weather conditions. Bring sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and a camera to capture the beautiful scenery and unique geological features.
